panels.title: Registered nodes

targets.expr: count(group by (node) (tip_node_registry_info))

panels.description:
**Registered nodes** - Total count of nodes registered in the DAG `nodes` table, regardless of their current online state.

**Healthy**: matches the operator's expected federation size. Equals the sum of online + offline registered nodes (see Federation Status table below).

**Trouble**: drift from expected count = unauthorized registration or missed deregistration.

panels.title: Online (scrapeable)

targets.expr: count(up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.description:
**Online (scrapeable)** - Count of nodes currently emitting metrics - `up{job="tip-federation"} == 1`. Reflects whether Prometheus can reach each node's `/metrics` endpoint, NOT whether they're healthy at the consensus layer.

**Healthy**: equals registered nodes (all online).

**Trouble**: < registered means at least one node is unreachable. Cross-check with the Federation Status table to identify which.

panels.title: Active committee (max)

targets.expr: max(tip_narwhal_active_participants and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1))

panels.description:
**Active committee (max)** - Largest active-committee value reported across all online nodes. Should be identical across all nodes since the committee is deterministically derived from DAG state.

**Healthy**: matches what each per-node tile shows below.

**Trouble**: differs from the per-node "Active committee size" panel = nodes computing different committees from the same DAG → split-brain on quorum denominator. Investigate immediately.

panels.title: Quorum threshold

targets.expr: max(tip_narwhal_quorum and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1))

panels.description:
**Quorum threshold** - The 2f+1 majority needed for Bullshark to commit an anchor, derived from the active committee size. For N nodes: quorum = floor(2N/3) + 1.

**Healthy**: stable value matching the active committee.

**Trouble**: changes unexpectedly = committee membership shifted (could be legitimate auto-derivation from `recent_producers` or a node being silently dropped).

panels.title: Consensus divergence (byzantine-fork canary)

targets.expr: sum(tip_consensus_divergence_total)

panels.description:
**Consensus divergence** - Cumulative count of byzantine-fork events: anti-entropy saw two peers at the same `committed_round` but with different `state_merkle_root`.

**Healthy**: 0. This counter SHOULD never tick. It's a canary, not a liveness signal.

**Trouble**: any value > 0 means two nodes have diverged on canonical state at the same round. This is a critical safety event - page immediately. Do NOT auto-resolve. Investigate which fork is correct, halt the wrong one.

panels.title: Consensus index - federation max
panels.description: Federation-wide max - what all healthy nodes converge to. Per-node breakdown is in the panel to the right.

targets.expr: max(tip_bullshark_consensus_index{node=~"$node"} and on(instance) up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.title: Anchor rate - federation max (anchors/min)
panels.description: Federation-wide max - what all healthy nodes converge to. Per-node breakdown is in the panel to the right.

targets.expr: max((rate(tip_bullshark_anchors_committed_total{node=~"$node"}[5m]) * 60) and on(instance) up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.title: Halt status

targets.expr: ((tip_consensus_halted{node=~"$node"} * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info)))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.description:
**Halt status** - 0 = consensus advancing normally; 1 = halted (no new commits for > 3× round timeout, default ~6s).

**Healthy**: 0 (OK) on all nodes.

**Trouble**: any node showing 1 (HALTED) means quorum is lost. Recovery is operational - bring more committee nodes online so 2f+1 quorum returns. While halted, this node returns 503 on POST/PUT under /v1/ (consensus-gate middleware) so clients retry instead of silently piling into mempool.

panels.title: Peers connected

targets.expr: ((tip_network_peers_authorized{node=~"$node"} * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info)))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.description:
**Peers connected** - Number of TIP-handshake-authorized peers this node currently sees.

**Healthy**: equals committee_size - 1 (this node sees all other committee members). For a 2-node federation, healthy = 1. For 3-node, healthy = 2.

**Yellow (1)**: partial mesh - at least one peer reachable but not all.

**Red (0)**: node is isolated. Submissions to this node won't propagate. Check: peer-discovery (#38), DirectPeers (#37), libp2p connectivity, firewall.

panels.title: Node uptime
panels.description:
**Node uptime** - Seconds since this node's process started (`tip_process_uptime_seconds`). Resets on process restart.

**Healthy**: continuously increasing; matches operator's expectation of when the node was last brought up. Rolling restarts during deploys are expected.

**Trouble**: unexpected reset = node crashed or was killed (OOM, panic, segfault). Cross-check with crash logs and the memory panel below - OOM is the most common cause of unexpected restarts.

targets.expr: (tip_process_uptime_seconds * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.title: Process memory (RSS)
panels.description:
**Process memory (RSS)** - Resident Set Size of each node's Node.js process (`tip_process_memory_rss_bytes`). Includes V8 heap, libp2p buffers, SQLite page cache, and native modules.

**Healthy**: stable or slowly growing; should plateau in the hundreds of MB on a healthy node. Brief spikes during snapshot install are expected.

**Trouble**: unbounded growth = memory leak. Likely candidates: cert GC misconfigured (`TIP_GC_DISABLED`), mempool evictor stuck, libp2p pubsub buffer not draining, or the ordered-cert cache exceeding `ORDERED_HASH_CACHE_SIZE`. RSS approaching system memory triggers the OOM killer - cross-check with the uptime panel.

targets.expr: (tip_process_memory_rss_bytes * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.title: Federation status - registered nodes, online state, join state, connections
panels.description: One row per TIP node in the DAG registry. **Status** = registered status from the DAG. **Online** = whether the node is currently emitting metrics. **Sync state** = `ready` (advancing normally) or `syncing` (catching up; suppresses round production). **Sync duration** = seconds since the node entered sync mode (- when ready). Color thresholds: blue < 60s (normal catch-up), orange 60-180s (slow), red > 180s (likely stuck - see #66). **Connected to** = comma-separated names of peers it currently sees. Empty cells for offline nodes. Pairs with the dedicated halt-status panel below - sync info shows the *cause* of a halt (stuck node), halt-status shows the *effect* (quorum unreachable).

targets.expr: group by (node, name, status) (tip_node_registry_info)
(up{job="tip-federation"} == 1) * on(instance) group_left(node) (group by (instance, node) (tip_process_uptime_seconds))
group by (node, peers) (last_over_time(tip_network_peer_list[10s]))
max by (node) (tip_narwhal_join_state_syncing * 2 + tip_narwhal_join_state_catching_up * 1) or max by (node) (tip_narwhal_syncing * 2)
max by (node) (tip_narwhal_sync_duration_seconds)
max by (node) (tip_narwhal_catching_up_duration_seconds) or max by (node) (vector(0))

panels.title: Last committed round

targets.expr: ((tip_bullshark_last_committed_round{node=~"$node"} * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info)))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.description:
**Last committed round** - The highest round at which Bullshark has finalized an anchor commit. Tracked in-memory by Bullshark, ticks every successful anchor (~2s on healthy network).

**Healthy**: continuously increasing on all nodes, with at most 1-2 round drift between nodes (Bullshark requires the next wave's votes before finalizing the previous wave's leader).

**Trouble**: stuck for > 6s on any node = halt; large drift between nodes = sync issue.

panels.title: Mempool depth

targets.expr: (tip_mempool_size{node=~"$node"} * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.description:
**Mempool depth** - Number of pending transactions waiting to be committed via Bullshark.

**Healthy on idle network**: 0 (no pending submissions).

**Healthy on active network**: briefly > 0, drains within ~2s as anchors commit.

**Trouble**: continuously growing - txs are being submitted faster than they're being committed. Could indicate a halt, slow-network committee, or a DOS pattern.

panels.title: Active committee size (per node)

targets.expr: ((tip_narwhal_active_participants{node=~"$node"} * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info)))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.description:
**Active committee size** - Number of nodes in the currently-derived consensus committee on each node. Committee derives from `registered_active ∩ producers_in_last_K_rounds`, so it auto-includes producing nodes and excludes silent ones.

**Healthy**: identical across all nodes (every node computes the same committee deterministically from its DAG state). Equal to the count of currently-producing registered nodes.

**Trouble**: drift between nodes means split-brain on quorum denominator - quorum calculations diverge, certs may not validate cross-node. A value of 0 means no quorum is possible (halt-gate will trip).

panels.title: Consensus index (anchor commits since genesis) - per node
panels.description:
**Consensus index** - Network-wide count of anchor commits. Persisted via `consensus_meta` (#44) so it survives restart on idle networks; joining nodes adopt the cluster's value at snapshot install time via `peer_consensus_index` (Cosmos/Sui pattern), so all caught-up nodes converge to the same value.

**Healthy**: all online nodes report the same value (modulo 1-2 of in-flight drift), monotonically increasing.

**Trouble**: a node's value frozen for > 3 minutes = consensus halted on that node. Sustained large absolute-value drift between nodes = a joiner didn't pick up snapshot-based convergence; check sync logs.

For commit rate, see the next panel (Consensus advance rate) - it's sourced from a per-process counter that's immune to snapshot-adoption jumps. Alert: `rate(tip_bullshark_anchors_committed_total[5m]) == 0 for: 3m`.

targets.expr: ((tip_bullshark_consensus_index{node=~"$node"} * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info)))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.title: Consensus advance rate (anchors/min) - per node
panels.description:
**Consensus advance rate (anchors/min)** - Rate at which Bullshark commits anchor certs, per minute, over a 5-minute window. Sourced from `tip_bullshark_anchors_committed_total` - a per-process counter that ticks only on real local anchor commits.

**Why not `rate(consensus_index)`?** `consensus_index` jumps when a joining node adopts the peer's network-wide value via `peer_consensus_index` (snapshot fast-sync). That one-step jump produces a spurious rate spike for ~5 min after each new joiner. The per-process anchor counter is immune to those jumps.

**Healthy**: ~25-30 anchors/min on every node, all within ~1/min of each other (Bullshark commits at ~2s cadence).

**Trouble**:
- Any node < 1/min for 3+ min = consensus halted on that node
- One node consistently lower than others = lagging, AE should auto-recover
- All nodes drop together = federation-wide halt (check halt-status panel)

Alertmanager: `rate(tip_bullshark_anchors_committed_total[5m]) == 0 for: 3m`.

targets.expr: ((rate(tip_bullshark_anchors_committed_total{node=~"$node"}[5m]) * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) * 60) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.title: Nodes online over time
panels.description:
Per-node scrape success (1 = online).

Normal: flat at 1 for all.
Act if: a line dips to 0, that node's thread stalled past the scrape timeout (correlate with the stall panel on the left).

targets.expr: up{job="tip-federation"}

panels.title: Narwhal current round

targets.expr: ((tip_narwhal_current_round{node=~"$node"} * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info)))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.description:
**Narwhal current round** - The round Narwhal is currently producing batches at. Always 1-2 rounds ahead of `last_committed_round` since Bullshark needs the next wave's votes before finalizing.

**Healthy**: linear monotonic growth on all nodes; convergent across the federation.

**Trouble**: flat line on any node = production stalled (likely halt-gate); large drift between nodes = sync gap that anti-entropy should auto-recover (#28/#46).

panels.title: Bullshark commits / 5s

targets.expr: (rate(tip_bullshark_anchors_committed_total{node=~"$node"}[1m]) * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.description:
**Bullshark commits / 5s** - Rate of anchor commits in 5-second windows.

**Healthy**: ~2-3 commits per 5s on a 2-second-round network. All nodes show the same rate.

**Trouble**: zero or sporadic - consensus is failing to make progress (check halt status + peer connectivity).

panels.title: Mempool size over time

targets.expr: (tip_mempool_size{node=~"$node"} * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.description:
**Mempool size over time** - Per-node pending-tx queue size, time series.

**Healthy on idle**: flat at 0.

**Healthy on bursts**: brief spikes that drain quickly.

**Trouble**: monotonically growing line - submissions outpacing commits. Investigate consensus liveness or rate-limit upstream.

panels.title: Txs committed (cumulative)

targets.expr: (tip_bullshark_txs_committed_total{node=~"$node"} * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.description:
**Txs committed (cumulative)** - Running total of transactions ordered through Bullshark since this process started.

**Healthy**: stairs-monotonically-up, matching activity level. Resets on process restart (Prometheus runtime counter).

**Trouble**: flat line during expected activity = consensus stalled or no submissions reaching the network.

panels.title: Anti-entropy: peers queried / gaps pulled

targets.expr: (rate(tip_antientropy_peers_queried_total{node=~"$node"}[1m]) * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)
(rate(tip_antientropy_gaps_pulled_total{node=~"$node"}[1m]) * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.description:
**Anti-entropy: peers queried / gaps pulled** - Anti-entropy is the pull-side safety net (#28) that runs every 4s, querying every authorized peer's sync-status and pulling missing certs (or via #46 falling back to snapshot if the gap exceeds gc_depth).

**Healthy**: peers_queried ticks at the AE cadence; gaps_pulled stays at 0 (push path delivers everything live).

**Trouble**: gaps_pulled > 0 sustained = push-path reliability issue (#26, #27); RPC failures = peer unreachable.

panels.title: Cert GC (rounds pruned)

targets.expr: (rate(tip_bullshark_gc_runs_total{node=~"$node"}[1m]) * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.description:
**Cert GC (rounds pruned)** - Cumulative count of certificate rows pruned by Bullshark's GC trigger (every `gc_interval_commits`, prunes certs older than `gc_depth` rounds).

**Healthy**: linearly growing, lagging the anchor commit rate by gc_depth rounds.

**Trouble**: stuck at 0 with high commit count = GC isn't firing (check `TIP_GC_DISABLED` env). Sudden huge jump = lots of certs pruned at once (after a long offline period).

panels.title: Mempool throughput (submit / drain rate)
panels.description:
**Mempool throughput** - Cumulative-counter rate of txs entering and leaving the mempool, per second.

- **received**: `rate(tip_mempool_received_total[1m])` - submit rate (txs accepted into the mempool)
- **drained**: `rate(tip_mempool_drained_total[1m])` - commit rate (txs pulled into Narwhal batches)

**Healthy**: drained tracks received closely on a busy network. On idle, both stay flat at 0.

**Trouble**:
- received > drained sustained = consensus can't keep up; mempool grows unbounded (see Mempool size over time)
- received > 0 with drained = 0 = halt-gate is engaged or batches aren't being pulled (#26/#27)
- drained > received = catching up on backlog (transient, healthy)

These counters fix the sampling blind spot of the gauge: gauge can return to 0 between scrapes; counters never miss transient flow.

targets.expr: (rate(tip_mempool_received_total{node=~"$node"}[1m]) * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)
(rate(tip_mempool_drained_total{node=~"$node"}[1m]) * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)
